_meta.description: Known ransomware operator wallets — documented in public threat-intelligence reports, DOJ press releases, CISA advisories. Used by the risk scorer to flag direct exposure to ransomware infrastructure. Each entry includes the operator name, attribution source, severity (always 4 = critical for ransomware), and applicable CISA advisory or DOJ docket where available.
_meta.source_caveat: Public attribution of ransomware addresses is messy. Sources cited here include CISA advisories, DOJ press releases, OFAC announcements, and reporting from Chainalysis / Elliptic / TRM public posts. Verify against the most recent advisory before acting; ransomware groups rotate infrastructure regularly. For active investigations, request the latest IOC list from your CISA / FBI liaison.

addresses._section: Conti ransomware (Russian-aligned, dissolved 2022 but successor groups active)

addresses.name: Conti — ransomware payment address (DOJ-cited)
addresses.notes: Identified in DOJ filings against Conti operators. Operations dissolved May 2022 after Conti leaks; member groups continue under different brands (BlackCat, Royal, etc.).

addresses.name: LockBit — affiliate payment address
addresses.notes: LockBit RaaS payment infrastructure. CISA advisory AA23-165A (June 2023). Multi-affiliate operation — many addresses; this is one of the documented ones.

addresses.name: BlackCat / ALPHV — payment address
addresses.notes: CISA advisory AA23-353A. BlackCat operations partially seized by FBI December 2023 but successor activity ongoing.

addresses.name: Royal — payment address
addresses.notes: CISA advisory AA23-061A. Royal succeeded Conti members in 2022; targets healthcare + critical infrastructure.

addresses._section: REvil / Sodinokibi (largely disrupted 2022 but historical activity remains)

addresses.name: REvil — historical payment address
addresses.notes: REvil core operators arrested in Russia January 2022. Historical addresses still surface in laundering chains.

addresses.name: DPRK Maui — ransomware against healthcare
addresses.notes: CISA + FBI + Treasury joint advisory AA22-187A. Maui ransomware attributed to DPRK state actors targeting U.S. healthcare. ALSO meets OFAC sanctioned criteria — any direct exposure is SDN-grade.
